resposta:
solution: ```class Solution:
    def lengthOfLongestSubstring(self, s: str) -> int:
        seen = {}
        left = 0
        max_len = 0
        for right, ch in enumerate(s):
            if ch in seen and seen[ch] >= left:
                left = seen[ch] + 1
            seen[ch] = right
            max_len = max(max_len, right - left + 1)
        return max_len
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(min(n, m)) where m is the size of the character set
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Sliding-window with a dictionary to store the last index of each character. When a duplicate is found within the current window, the left boundary jumps just past the previous occurrence, ensuring O(n) single pass.
